title: Align host/proposal stochastic graph kernels at g=0
description: Make AdaptiveWise and ProposalAdaptiveWise use one distribution-preserving multinomial helper so a proposal equal to the host core p1 has identical sample_nonpreference/sample_prob draws and RNG consumption, while non-constant per-row proposal probabilities retain batched sampling.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the shared sampler helper is tested on an identical row-constant proposal THEN host and proposal sample_nonpreference and sample_prob return identical tensors and leave identical RNG states under a fixed seed; WHEN a non-constant proposal matrix is tested THEN each row is sampled from its own probability vector with no flattening shortcut; WHEN deterministic rate/prob/reverse kernels receive equal p1/proposal rows THEN existing exact-value behavior remains unchanged; HARD STOP: any change to seed 100, FP32 tolerance, optimizer membership, model parameters, proposal values, evaluator, or corruption is forbidden, and a new dated remote E1 attempt must fail closed if any checkpoint diverges; ref: graph_lib.py:1203

title: Instrument per-stage RNG provenance for E1
description: Record pre/post RNG metadata around each initial validation sampler call and its first batch so the remaining R3 step-0 RNG mismatch is localized without changing production behavior.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the diagnostic trace runs THEN initialization evidence contains per-arm RNG metadata for construction, normalized trace start, validation-loader iterator creation, each p2 sampler call, and post-evaluation capture with call counts and batch shapes; WHEN the diagnostic compares arms THEN it identifies the first stage where RNG metadata diverges and preserves all existing model/optimizer/proposal values; HARD STOP: instrumentation must be behavior-preserving, seed 100/trace steps/tolerance remain frozen, no result is treated as pass, and no downstream training launches from this diagnostic row; ref: scripts/run_e01_gzero_trace.py:1113
review_initial_requirements: Verify the diagnostic wraps the existing evaluator/sampler without consuming extra RNG or altering output, and use R3 evidence to target the stage boundary.
review_regression_requirements: Verify focused instrumentation tests, full regression suite, isolated bundle hash, and one remote diagnostic trace; classify the first divergent stage before selecting R5 fix.

title: Run a new dated E1 production trace attempt
description: Execute one fresh isolated production-path E1 g-zero trace after the prior attempt failed before trace entry because its bundle omitted catsample.py; preserve the prior failure as historical evidence and do not retry or overwrite it.
acceptance_criteria: WHEN the new dated attempt runs with random_seed 100 THEN proposal rows, loss terms, gradients, parameters, optimizer state, ordered batch IDs, configuration, and RNG metadata are compared at trace steps 0, 1, 100, and 1000 under FP32 tolerance 1e-6; WHEN the attempt reaches a terminal state THEN exactly one dated e01_gzero_trace.json records pass or fail, first divergence, hashes, log, and isolated paths; HARD STOP: any preflight/import/execution error or first tracked difference above tolerance fails this attempt closed, creates no RISK-02 pass marker, launches no training, and permits no retry, rescue tuning, threshold change, or corruption change; only a valid pass may unlock the approved seed100 continuation path; ref: scripts/run_e01_gzero_trace.py:1
review_initial_requirements: Verify this is a genuinely new dated attempt with a complete runtime bundle including catsample.py, fixed seed/steps/tolerance, no pre-created output directory, GPU1 isolation, and no mutation of frozen artifacts.
review_regression_requirements: Verify terminal artifact identity and hashes, distinguish preflight/execution failure from scientific divergence, preserve the old attempt, and confirm downstream launch remains locked unless E1 pass plus RISK-02 marker and controller gates validate.
